entries.summary: Use a 3:2:2 creative testing structure — 3 hooks, 2 body copy variations, 2 CTAs — within a single ad set to isolate what variable is actually driving performance.
entries.key_takeaway: Never change more than one creative element at a time. Test hooks first, then body, then CTA.
entries.comment_notes: Multiple media buyers confirmed this framework reduces wasted spend on bad creative
entries.source_text: Stop testing 10 creatives at once. Use 3:2:2 — 3 hooks, 2 bodies, 2 CTAs. You'll know exactly what moved the needle.

entries.summary: Broad targeting with strong creative outperforms tight interest targeting for most ecommerce accounts above $100/day spend. Let the algorithm find your buyers.
entries.key_takeaway: Remove interest targeting and run broad (age/gender only) once you have 50+ purchases in pixel history.
entries.comment_notes: Strong consensus in comments — multiple buyers reporting 20-40% ROAS improvement switching to broad
entries.source_text: Broad targeting is outperforming interest stacking for 80% of ecom accounts I work with right now. Your creative IS your targeting.

entries.summary: Creative fatigue sets in when frequency exceeds 3.5-4x for cold audiences. Signs: CTR drops 30%+ while CPM stays flat. Fix: pause the ad, wait 2 weeks, relaunch with a new hook.
entries.key_takeaway: Monitor frequency weekly. Above 4x on cold audiences means new creative needed, not more budget.
entries.comment_notes: Universally agreed — frequency 4+ is the most common reason for unexplained ROAS decline
entries.source_text: Frequency above 4 on cold audiences = creative fatigue. Stop throwing budget at it. Pause, refresh, relaunch.

entries.summary: Advantage+ Shopping Campaigns (ASC) are outperforming manual campaign structures for most ecommerce stores. Meta's algorithm allocates budget between prospecting and retargeting automatically.
entries.key_takeaway: Test ASC alongside your existing structure. Give it 2 weeks and same budget. Most accounts see 15-30% efficiency gain.
entries.comment_notes: Mostly positive but some accounts with niche products report worse results — works best for broad-appeal products
entries.source_text: ASC is the real deal for ecom. Tested it on 12 accounts — 9 of them saw significant efficiency improvements vs manual structure.

entries.summary: The first 3 seconds of a video ad determine 80% of watch-through. Pattern interrupt hooks (unexpected visuals, bold claims, direct questions) consistently outperform polished brand openers.
entries.key_takeaway: Test 3+ different hooks on the same video body. The hook is the highest-leverage creative variable.
entries.comment_notes: Overwhelming agreement — hook testing is universally cited as highest ROI creative activity
entries.source_text: 80% of your ad's success is decided in the first 3 seconds. I test 5 hooks per video minimum. Same body, different opener. The winner is never what you expect.

entries.summary: Cost cap bidding gives you control over CPA but requires enough conversion history. Use it only after you have 30+ purchases at target CPA with lowest cost bidding first.
entries.key_takeaway: Don't use cost cap from day 1. Earn it with lowest cost first, then switch once the algorithm has data.
entries.comment_notes: Consensus that cost cap kills delivery if used too early — needs conversion history to work
entries.source_text: Cost cap without conversion history = under-delivery. Get 30+ purchases at lowest cost first. THEN switch to cost cap to protect margins at scale.

entries.summary: UGC (user-generated content) style ads consistently outperform polished studio ads for DTC ecommerce. Authenticity signals trust. Aim for content that looks native to the feed.
entries.key_takeaway: If your ad looks like an ad, it performs like an ad. Native-looking UGC with real customer language outperforms brand video 70% of the time.
entries.comment_notes: Strong agreement across ecom niche — UGC winning in most accounts
entries.source_text: Your polished brand video is losing to someone's iPhone selfie. UGC wins because it doesn't look like an ad. Native = trust = conversions.

entries.summary: Retargeting works best with short windows (1-7 days) and personalized creative referencing what the user saw. Long retargeting windows (30-90 days) often waste spend on people who have already decided not to buy.
entries.key_takeaway: Use 7-day view/click windows for retargeting. Show cart abandoners the specific product they viewed, not a generic brand ad.
entries.comment_notes: Consistent reports of 30-day windows underperforming 7-day for most ecom categories
entries.source_text: 90-day retargeting audiences are mostly people who decided no. Tighten to 7 days and show them the exact product. Retargeting ROAS doubles.
